visualGoal: A wiring how-to with honestly low product density. The ONE verified visual (Honeycomb Bravo) exists to illustrate WHY plain USB fails — a backlit, controller-driven panel is the power draw problem in a box. The subject of the article (the USB hub itself) has no verified catalog image, so it is carried by text + official/Amazon links. Do not force product cards; the wiring/port math table and callouts carry the page.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The actual subject of the article. No catalog image exists. Sourcing a real photo of this 6-port 30W hub would let the hub become a body visual (gearpick/shotgrid) and raise the verified-image count above 1. Highest-value photo to source for this article.

A file exists at this path on disk but it is NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it must not be embedded. Referenced text-only for the 'buy one tier more ports' point. Price is now ~$108 (flight-extra.json says ~$60 — stale). Verify a real product photo before any body embed, and update the price in flight-extra.json.

Referenced in the 'note on force feedback' aside as text link only. Image file exists on disk but is NOT in the verified list, so it is not embedded. If it becomes verified, could anchor the FFB aside.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Clean home flight-sim cockpit desk from above at soft key light, a self-powered USB hub as the central node with tidy labeled cables branching to a yoke, throttle quadrant and stacked backlit airliner panels, cable tray visible, no fake logos, no fake UI, an open flat area left for a real product overlay, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake brand logos, invented hub model, warped ports, impossible cabling, unreadable tiny text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: What draws power in a cockpit
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, flight blues, real Bravo/Alpha photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Powered vs bus-powered — the one that matters
content: Bus-powered = shares one port's 500/900mA — browns out panels
Self-powered = own wall adapter (30W / 60W) — the right tool
infographicPrompts.style: Two-column decision chart, flight colors, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan at a tidy home cockpit desk, one hand on a powered USB hub, calm methodical builder energy, labeled cables in frame, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: Screenshot of Windows Power Options advanced settings showing 'USB selective suspend setting → Disabled', and Device Manager USB Root Hub Power Management tab. Would strengthen the two-step dropout fix section.
